name: Activate The Existing 128x128 Two-Stage Hot-Band Kernel

summary.name: Activate The Existing 128x128 Two-Stage Hot-Band Kernel
summary.hypothesis: The staged K32 family is now closed-negative, but the simpler 128x128 two-stage sibling is still a distinct PTX-adjacent family that can reduce barrier and scoreboard pressure while keeping the restored hot-band baseline structure intact.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Residual synchronization and latency-hiding overhead in the hot-band steady state, with lower occupancy pressure than the failed K32 staging variant.
summary.risk: Medium. It is adjacent to the current restored baseline and may still inherit the same launch geometry limits, but it is materially different from the closed K32 staged family.
summary.stop_condition: Keep this family only if the next measured run beats the restored PTX baseline by a clear margin or at least moves barrier/scoreboard stalls back toward the round-78 profile; otherwise close it and move to a bounded cleanup family.

notes: Node C may now implement this one direction.
